workItems.reason: No CPR action card exists in the library. Only a cpr-reference (reference_guide) is present in the Airway Emergencies parent topic. Cardiac arrest CPR is the most critical missing action card — it is the foundational life-safety skill for an unresponsive, non-breathing adult. The existing cpr-reference covers background knowledge only; there is no step-by-step guide to walk someone through compressions in an emergency.
workItems.notes: Scope: adult bystander CPR (hands-only primary, rescue breaths for trained), AED integration, child note. Source: AHA 2020 CPR/ECC guidelines, Red Cross. Do NOT scope to infant CPR — that warrants a separate guide.

duplicateRisks.overlapDescription: Both are in Airway Emergencies and cover CPR. The reference guide provides background; the new action card provides emergency steps. These are complementary, not duplicates.
duplicateRisks.recommendation: Keep both. Differentiate clearly: action card for the emergency moment, reference guide for deeper reading.

duplicateRisks.overlapDescription: Choking-unresponsive ends with CPR if the person stops breathing. There is overlap in the final steps.
duplicateRisks.recommendation: Keep both. The choking-unresponsive guide covers the choking-specific sequence; the CPR action card covers the cardiac arrest scenario. Cross-reference via alternativeToGuideSlugs.
